---
title: "partner2"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
movies <- read.csv("../data/cleaned_movies.csv")

str(movies)
summary(movies)
colSums(is.na(movies))
```

```{r}
# Convert categorical variables and create log transforms
movies$IsVideoGame <- as.factor(movies$IsVideoGame)
movies$IsSpringRelease <- as.factor(movies$IsSpringRelease)

movies$logDomestic <- log(movies$DomesticTotal)
movies$logPrevDomestic <- log(movies$PrevFilmDomestic)
movies$logBudget <- log(movies$Budget)

movies$SequelRatio <- movies$DomesticTotal / movies$PrevFilmDomestic
```

```{r}
# Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

# Raw relationship
plot(movies$PrevFilmDomestic, movies$DomesticTotal,
     xlab = "Previous Film Domestic",
     ylab = "Sequel Domestic",
     main = "Domestic vs Previous Film")

# Log-log (important)
plot(movies$logPrevDomestic, movies$logDomestic,
     xlab = "log(Previous Domestic)",
     ylab = "log(Domestic)",
     main = "Log-Log Relationship")

# Sequel ratios
hist(movies$SequelRatio,
     main = "Sequel Ratio Distribution",
     xlab = "Sequel / Previous")

# Category comparisons
boxplot(DomesticTotal ~ IsVideoGame, data = movies)
boxplot(DomesticTotal ~ IsSpringRelease, data = movies)
```

Build Models

```{r}
# baseline

model1 <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic, data = movies)
summary(model1)
```

```{r}
# add Year

model2 <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year, data = movies)
summary(model2)

anova(model1, model2)
```

```{r}
# add video game

model3 <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year + IsVideoGame, data = movies)
summary(model3)

anova(model2, model3)
```

```{r}
# add spring release

model4 <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year + IsSpringRelease, data = movies)
summary(model4)

anova(model2, model4)
```

```{r}
# add budget

model5 <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year + logBudget, data = movies)
summary(model5)

anova(model2, model5)
```


```{r}
# Model selection

full_model <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year + logBudget +
                   IsVideoGame + IsSpringRelease, data = movies)

step_model <- step(full_model, direction = "both", trace = 0)
summary(step_model)
```

```{r}
# Final model

final_model <- model5
```

```{r}
# Diagnostics

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(final_model)

cooksD <- cooks.distance(final_model)
plot(cooksD, type = "h")
abline(h = 4/nrow(movies), lty = 2)
```

```{r}
# Sensitivity Analysis

influential_index <- which.max(cooksD)

movies_no_outlier <- movies[-influential_index, ]

model_no_outlier <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year,
                       data = movies_no_outlier)

coef(final_model)
coef(model_no_outlier)
```

```{r}
# Predict Mario Galaxy

new_mario <- data.frame(
  logPrevDomestic = log(574930000),   # Mario 1
  Year = 2026,
  logBudget = log(180000000),         # estimate
  IsVideoGame = factor(1, levels = levels(movies$IsVideoGame)),
  IsSpringRelease = factor(1, levels = levels(movies$IsSpringRelease))
)
```

predict:
```{r}
pred_log <- predict(final_model, newdata = new_mario, interval = "prediction")
pred_log
```

convert back to dollars:
```{r}
exp(pred_log)
```



```{r}
# =========================
# Partner 2: Accuracy / Calibration Code
# =========================

movies <- read.csv("../data/cleaned_movies.csv")

# Prepare variables
movies$IsVideoGame <- as.factor(movies$IsVideoGame)
movies$IsSpringRelease <- as.factor(movies$IsSpringRelease)

movies$logDomestic <- log(movies$DomesticTotal)
movies$logPrevDomestic <- log(movies$PrevFilmDomestic)
movies$logBudget <- log(movies$Budget)
movies$SequelRatio <- movies$DomesticTotal / movies$PrevFilmDomestic

# -------------------------
# 1. Final regression model
# -------------------------
final_model <- lm(logDomestic ~ logPrevDomestic + Year + logBudget, data = movies)
summary(final_model)

# -------------------------
# 2. Predict Mario Galaxy total domestic
# -------------------------
new_mario <- data.frame(
  logPrevDomestic = log(574930000),   # Mario 1 domestic gross
  Year = 2026,
  logBudget = log(180000000)          # estimated budget
)

pred_log <- predict(final_model, newdata = new_mario, interval = "prediction")
pred_total <- exp(pred_log)

cat("Regression-based TOTAL domestic prediction:\n")
print(pred_total)

# -------------------------
# 3. Sequel ratio method
# -------------------------
ratio_mean <- mean(movies$SequelRatio)
ratio_median <- median(movies$SequelRatio)

mario1_domestic <- 574930000

ratio_total_mean <- mario1_domestic * ratio_mean
ratio_total_median <- mario1_domestic * ratio_median

cat("\nSequel-ratio TOTAL domestic prediction:\n")
cat("Using mean ratio:   $", round(ratio_total_mean, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Using median ratio: $", round(ratio_total_median, 0), "\n", sep = "")

# -------------------------
# 4. Industry / sanity-check method
# -------------------------
# This is a manual realistic range based on comparable sequel behavior.
industry_total_low  <- 500000000
industry_total_high <- 580000000
industry_total_mid  <- (industry_total_low + industry_total_high) / 2

cat("\nIndustry/sanity-check TOTAL domestic range:\n")
cat("Low:  $", industry_total_low, "\n", sep = "")
cat("Mid:  $", round(industry_total_mid, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("High: $", industry_total_high, "\n", sep = "")

# -------------------------
# 5. Convert TOTAL -> May 1 domestic
# -------------------------
# Assume about 90% of total is earned by May 1
may1_share <- 0.90

reg_may1_fit <- pred_total[, "fit"] * may1_share
reg_may1_lwr <- pred_total[, "lwr"] * may1_share
reg_may1_upr <- pred_total[, "upr"] * may1_share

ratio_may1_mean   <- ratio_total_mean * may1_share
ratio_may1_median <- ratio_total_median * may1_share

industry_may1_low  <- industry_total_low * may1_share
industry_may1_mid  <- industry_total_mid * may1_share
industry_may1_high <- industry_total_high * may1_share

cat("\nMay 1 domestic estimates:\n")
cat("Regression fit:      $", round(reg_may1_fit, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Regression PI low:   $", round(reg_may1_lwr, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Regression PI high:  $", round(reg_may1_upr, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Ratio mean method:   $", round(ratio_may1_mean, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Ratio median method: $", round(ratio_may1_median, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Industry low:        $", round(industry_may1_low, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Industry mid:        $", round(industry_may1_mid, 0), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Industry high:       $", round(industry_may1_high, 0), "\n", sep = "")

# -------------------------
# 6. Weighted final prediction
# -------------------------
# Put less weight on regression because it looks inflated / unstable
# Suggested weights:
# 30% regression, 35% sequel-ratio median, 35% industry midpoint

# Adjust ratio downward (important!)
adjusted_ratio <- 0.90   # realistic sequel behavior

ratio_total_adjusted <- mario1_domestic * adjusted_ratio
ratio_may1_adjusted <- ratio_total_adjusted * may1_share

# New weights (less regression influence)
final_submission <- 0.05 * reg_may1_fit +
                    0.50 * ratio_may1_adjusted +
                    0.45 * industry_may1_mid

cat("\nWeighted FINAL May 1 prediction:\n")
cat("$", round(final_submission, 0), "\n", sep = "")

# -------------------------
# 7. Scenario table
# -------------------------
low_case  <- mean(c(reg_may1_lwr, ratio_may1_median * 0.95, industry_may1_low))
base_case <- final_submission
high_case <- mean(c(reg_may1_fit * 0.95, ratio_may1_mean, industry_may1_high))

scenario_table <- data.frame(
  Scenario = c("Low", "Base", "High"),
  Prediction = round(c(low_case, base_case, high_case), 0)
)

cat("\nScenario table:\n")
print(scenario_table)

# -------------------------
# 8. Recommended rounded submission
# -------------------------
recommended_submission <- round(final_submission / 1000000) * 1000000

cat("\nRecommended rounded submission:\n")
cat("$", recommended_submission, "\n", sep = "")
```
